title: Trump does not rule out sending US troops into Iran
subhead: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that the country could sustain the assault on Iran beyond the month-long timeframe he has publicly spoken about. 'The big wave hasn't even happened,' he said.

title: Macron says France to increase nuclear warheads
subhead: French President Macron spoke after the launch of US and Israeli attacks against Iran in a campaign that risks destabilizing the Middle East. 'An upgrade of our arsenal is essential,' he said.

title: Israel unites against the 'existential threat' of Iran: 'We need to get rid of this regime that hates us'
subhead: With schools, non-food shopping centers and restaurants closed, Israelis have been living since Saturday under air raid alerts and Iranian missile strikes. These attacks have already claimed several lives.

title: From the UAE to Qatar, Gulf countries condemn 'Iranian aggression'
subhead: Iran's reprisals against the territories of various Gulf monarchies expose the failure of recent efforts to appease Iran and highlight the limits of the American security umbrella.

title: Iranian regime scrambles to survive after Khamenei's death
subhead: Amid scenes of celebration and mourning, the formation of an interim government and ongoing bombings, Iran has entered a period of profound uncertainty, where the Islamic Republic's very survival and the reshaping of its internal balance of power are at stake.

Attacks on Iran: US and Israel are committing 'a crime of aggression,' says Bernie Sanders' former adviser
subhead: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'

title: France-Germany-UK's balancing act in response to US-Israeli offensive against Iran
subhead: In a joint statement indicating a form of tacit support without offensive participation, France, Germany and the United Kingdom stressed that they had not participated in the strikes against Tehran and criticized the Iranian attacks against countries in the region.

title: The 'great replacement' theory: 'A hateful, conspiratorial phrase' now in 'everyday language'
subhead: The expression, coined by far-right ideologue Renaud Camus in 2010, is based on a myth Hitler developed. Its drift into everyday language marks a turning point, according to Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard.
